english: taste or smell of it agreeable; śa ₒdi lhág-par źim-na (sha 'di lhag par zhim na) this meat being of a better taste Dzl.; źim-rgyui (zhim rgyui) zas food prepared of savoury things Zam.; dri-źim (dri zhim), dri-bsuṅ źim-po (dri bsung zhim po) pleasant odour Dzl.; dri mi źim-pa (dri mi zhim pa) disagreeable smell Glr.; *źim-ze* (*zhim ze*) also *źim-źim* (*zhim zhim*) C, *źim-zag* (*zhim zag*) W. sweet-meats, confectionery; *źim-zag-tsóṅ-kán* (*zhim zag tshong mkhan*) W. confectioner; *źim-lto-ćan* (*zhim lto can*) W. dainty-mouthed, a sweet-tooth.

amendment, improvement, correction; the word is also added at the end of written books, e.g. of Taranatha, as an attestation of a careful revision; źu-dág byéd-pa (zhu dag byed pa) to mend, improve, correct; raṅ-rgyúd źu-dág byéd-pa (rang rgyud zhu dag byed pa) to examine and reform one's self Cs. źu-dag-mkán (zhu dag mkhan) reviser, corrector, censor Cs., źu-ćén-gyi lóts-tsa-ba (zhu chen gyi lo tsA ba) a great corrector or commentator (of Ssk. writings), seems to have become a current title.

I. vb., pf. źus (zhus) (esp. in later writings and vulgo, in ancient literature gen. ysól-bar (gsol bar) for it) signifies 1. every kind of speaking to a person of higher rank, therefore to request, to prefer a suit or petition, to make a report, to put a question etc., źú-źiṅ ysól-ba-la (zhu zhing gsol ba la) ₒgró-bai tśe ('gro bai tshe) when I have to bring in a petition Dzl.; ₒmnál-lam de yzán-la mi ysúṅ-bar źu' ('mnal lam de gzhan la mi gsung bar zhu') źus-so (zhus so) 'pray, do not relate the dream to others', he begged Glr.; ynáṅ-bar źu (gnang bar zhu) byás-pas (byas pas) saying, 'I beg you will permit', Glr.; sṅar mtóṅ-bai dṅós-po dé-dag źuo (sngar mthong bai dngos po de dag zhuo) I will ask him about the things lately seen, I shall request an explanation of him Dzl.; ṅá-la gáṅ-dag źu-ba de légs-so (nga la gang dag zhu ba de legs so) it is very right of you, thus to ask me about every thing Do.; rgyál-poi drúṅ-du rmi-lam źus-pa (rgyal poi drung du rmi lam zhus pa) he related the dream before the king Pth; ston-pa źu (ston pa zhu) (pa col. for par) I request (you) to explain Mil.; der ₒbyón-pa źu (der 'byon pa zhu) 'thither to come I request' Mil.; dé-la mkán-po źus (de la mkhan po zhus) they besought him to be their abbot Glr.; ynáṅ-ba źu-ba (gnang ba zhu ba) to ask permission Cs. — 2. In W. this źu (zhu) has become a word of civility to the widest extent, as it is not only added to almost every sentence of a speech or a letter, something like our 'with your permission' or 'if you please', e.g. *źan ći méd-na ṅa ḍo yin źu* (*gzhan ci med na nga 'gro yin zhu*) if you have nothing further (to say), I shall go, with your permission; *ko leb soṅ źu* (*kho sleb song zhu*) he is arrived, if you please; but it also supplies every kind of salutation in coming or going, hence *źu zér-će* (*zhu zer ce*), resp. *źu źú-će* (*zhu zhu ce*), to make or give one's compliments, *á-pa-ne źu máṅ-po źu dug* (*a pha ne zhu mang po zhu dug*) my father's best respects (cf. p̣yag (phyag)). Inst. of źu (zhu), j́u (ju) is also frq. heard (vulgo), e.g. *j́u sab j́u!* (*ju sab ju!*) good day, Sir, good day! which prob. is only an intension of sound, and not to be referred to the Indian जी (ji). — *ći-la źu* (*ci la zhu*) W. why, well then, mind! *ći-la źu, nyi-ráṅ ṅe tsur ma kyód-pa yun-ríṅ kyód* (*ci la zhu, nyi rang nge tshur ma bskyod pa yun ring bskyod*) well, I have not seen you this age! — The word is also used as a sbst., for request, wish, question, *źú-wa búl-ba* (*zhu wa 'bul ba*) C., *púl-će* (*phul ce*) W., ytóṅ-ba (gtong ba) Glr. to make a request, to put a question; ydan-ₒdrén-pai źu-ba nán-ćan púl-bas (gdan 'dren pai zhu ba nan can phul bas) Mil. assailing him with pressing invitations. II. (prop. fut. of ₒjú-ba ('ju ba)) pf. (b)źu(s) ((b)zhu(s)), fut. (b)źu ((b)zhu), (imp.?) 1. to melt, trs. and intrs., bźu-btúl (bzhu btul) v. sub lugs (lugs); bźu-byai yser (bzhu byai gser) gold to be melted Cs.; źú-bai kams (zhu bai khams) whatever is melting or fusible, metals Sch.; źuo (zhuo) it melted (from the heat) Dzl.; ₒód-du źu-nas ('od du zhu nas) dissolving in light Glr. frq. — 2. to digest, źú-byed-kyi sman (zhu byed kyi sman) digestive medicine Cs. (cf. ₒju-byéd ('ju byed)); ma-źú(-ba) (ma zhu (-ba)) undigested, zas ma źú-ba (zas ma zhu ba) undigested food, also indigestion, sufferings arising from it; ma-źúi nad (ma zhui nad) id.; ma-źu ₒjú-ba (ma zhu 'ju ba) to decompose what is undigested Med.; opp. to źu-rjés (zhu rjes) it seems to denote more particularly the chyme before it is mixed with bile, and perh. also the duodenum where this takes place; so the region of źu (zhu) daṅ (dang) ma-źúi bár-na (ma zhui bar na) is stated to be the place, where the bile is principally operating S.g. Cf. ₒjú-ba ('ju ba). Comp. źu-skyogs (zhu skyogs) W. crucible, melting-spoon. — źu-mkán (zhu mkhan) 1. petitioner. 2. digester; n. of an officinal plant, = spaṅ-źuṅ (spang zhung) Wdṅ. (Wdng.).
